summary: In this deep and honest conversation, Bhartendra Singh sits down with Thomas Priju, a portfolio manager with 25+ years of experience across Mutual Funds, PMS, Hedge Funds, and AIFs. This episode breaks down how fund managers really think, how alpha is created, how sector cycles work, and why most investors fail to build long-term wealth.

seo.title: How Fund Managers Really Think: Stock Picking, Sector Cycles & Alpha Explained | Thomas Priju
seo.description: In this deep and honest conversation, Bhartendra Singh sits down with Thomas Priju, a portfolio manager with 25+ years of experience across Mutual Funds,

takeaways.text: Thomas Priju brings 25 plus years across mutual funds, PMS, hedge funds and AIFs, so this is a look at how fund managers actually think, not how they pitch themselves.
Alpha is framed as the real engine of returns, but the conversation is honest that stock picking is extremely hard, which is why sector cycles and business inflection points get so much weight.
IPOs get heavily oversubscribed even when the company is losing money, a reminder that hype and demand can run well ahead of the fundamentals.
Judging a fund manager fairly takes time, and the episode leans on a five-year rule rather than reacting to a few quarters of underperformance.
Patience, humility and process are treated as the traits that separate good investors, more than any single clever call.
